core_mechanism: bottom-up agent-based (individual-based) simulation: a population of many discrete interacting units evolving under explicitly specified local interaction rules, so that aggregate system-level behavior emerges from the micro-level interactions rather than being imposed by a closed-form macro law
brief_reason: Being a survey chapter it has no single sharp method, but its whole trajectory is anchored on agents that 'interact according to explicitly defined behavioral rules' to investigate general mechanisms, i.e. emergent macro-behavior generated from rule-based micro-level agent interactions.
